domain: Lithuanian sutartines polyphony (3-voice canon at second-interval ~180 cents dissonance; interlocking phase-staggered entries; texture loss if any voice removed)
specific_mechanism: K voices each performing the same melodic substrate but at phase-staggered entry; near-duplicate but offset; deliberate second-interval dissonance prevents acoustic fusion into a single line.
llm_application: SUTARTINE-KV: partition the KV cache into K interleaved sub-caches (e.g., K=3) where sub-cache i stores keys offset by phase shift Δi in embedding space (near-duplicate but second-interval-offset). Attention reads across all K sub-caches simultaneously; a 'dissonance penalty' suppresses scores where two sub-cache keys are within ε of each other (near-collision), enforcing K-fold compositional decoding. Removing any sub-cache breaks coherence — explicit redundancy with deliberate near-duplicate-suppression.
novelty_claim: Multi-Head Latent Attention (MLA) compresses to latent KV; GQA groups query heads; PyramidKV/CAKE allocate cache by depth-funneling; MorphKV selectively retains tokens; Recursion-Wise KV / KV Cache Steering manipulate cache content. SUTARTINE-KV differs by storing K NEAR-DUPLICATE phase-offset copies with explicit dissonance penalty enforcing K-fold compositional reads — neither compression nor selection but redundant-staggered structure.
